tasks.name: Analyze and Map Current Email Template Structure
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive mapping of all email templates, identifying HTML tags to remove, URL/email placeholders to eliminate, and standardizing placeholder format across templates. This includes examining both regular templates and event-related templates with different placeholder styles.
tasks.notes: This task focuses on analysis only, not implementation. The resulting map will guide the SQL migration creation in subsequent tasks.

relatedFiles.description: Directory containing existing migrations that define email templates

tasks.implementationGuide: 1. Use grep_search to identify all email templates in the database 2. Extract a list of all HTML anchor tags (...) 3. Identify all URL/email placeholders (like [signin_link], [profile_page_link]) 4. Document placeholder formats (both [placeholder] and {{placeholder}} styles) 5. Create a mapping document showing before/after changes for each template
tasks.verificationCriteria: Success criteria: Complete mapping document that lists all email templates, identifies all HTML elements and URL/email placeholders to remove, and standardizes the placeholder format.
tasks.analysisResult: The goal of this project is to simplify the email notification system by removing all URLs, emails, and clickable elements, keeping only plain text placeholders for essential data. This requires updates to email templates, SQL functions that create notification payloads, and edge functions that process these notifications.
tasks.summary: Task completed successfully. I created a comprehensive mapping of all email templates in the Notification System Cleanup project document. This includes identifying the email template structure, documenting URL and email placeholders found in templates, noting the mustache-style syntax used, and listing HTML elements to be removed. I also cataloged all affected templates that need to be updated. This information provides a clear roadmap for creating the SQL migration in subsequent tasks.

tasks.name: Analyze SQL Functions Creating Notifications
tasks.description: Examine and document all SQL functions that create notification payloads. Identify URL/email fields in payloads that need to be removed while preserving essential data fields like names, dates, and amounts.
tasks.notes: This task focuses on analysis only, not implementation. The resulting documentation will guide the SQL function updates in subsequent tasks.

relatedFiles.description: Directory containing migrations with SQL function definitions

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Analyze these SQL functions: create_deadline_notifications, handle_payment_notification, handle_payment_reported, handle_profile_verification_change, handle_verification_request_status_change, notify_organizer_new_submission, notify_submission_status_change, queue_trial_expiry_notification
2. Document current payload structure for each function
3. Identify URL/email fields to remove (links, contact emails, etc.)
4. Map out the revised payload structure keeping only essential data fields
tasks.verificationCriteria: Success criteria: Complete documentation of all SQL functions that create notifications, with clear identification of fields to remove and fields to retain.
tasks.analysisResult: The goal of this project is to simplify the email notification system by removing all URLs, emails, and clickable elements, keeping only plain text placeholders for essential data. This requires updates to email templates, SQL functions that create notification payloads, and edge functions that process these notifications.
tasks.summary: Task completed successfully. I analyzed all SQL functions that create notification payloads and documented their current structure, identified fields to remove (only support_email in handle_payment_notification), and fields to retain. The analysis was documented in NotificationSystemCleanup.md with detailed information for each function including code snippets showing the current payload structure. This analysis will guide the implementation of changes in subsequent tasks.

tasks.name: Update send-email Edge Function
tasks.description: Modify the send-email edge function to remove special URL handling logic and simplify it to process only plain text replacements. This ensures compatibility with the updated templates and notification payloads.
tasks.notes: This update should be done first to ensure the system can handle the updated templates and payloads.

relatedFiles.description: Contains the send-email edge function implementation

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Examine the current send-email function implementation
2. Identify and remove the URL detection logic that checks if a replacement value is a URL and transforms it into a clickable link
3. Update the placeholder processing regex if needed to match the standardized format
4. Test the function with sample notifications to ensure it correctly processes plain text replacements
tasks.verificationCriteria: Success criteria: The updated send-email function successfully processes placeholders without special URL handling, maintaining compatibility with the notification system.
tasks.analysisResult: The goal of this project is to simplify the email notification system by removing all URLs, emails, and clickable elements, keeping only plain text placeholders for essential data. This requires updates to email templates, SQL functions that create notification payloads, and edge functions that process these notifications.
tasks.summary: Successfully updated the send-email Edge Function by removing the special URL handling logic that previously converted URLs into clickable links. Modified the function to process all placeholders as plain text replacements without any special handling for URLs. Added documentation about these changes at the top of the file. Updated the NotificationSystemCleanup.md file to mark this task as completed and documented the changes made.

tasks.name: Create SQL Migration for Email Templates
tasks.description: Create a SQL migration to update all email templates in the database, removing HTML anchor tags, URL/email placeholders, and standardizing the placeholder format.
tasks.notes: This task depends on the analysis from Task 1, as it implements the changes identified there.

relatedFiles.description: Directory where the new migration file will be created

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Use the mapping document from Task 1 to guide template updates
2. Create a new migration file in the supabase/migrations directory
3. For each template:
   - Remove all <a href="..."> tags and any click instructions
   - Remove URL/email placeholders
   - Standardize remaining placeholders to [placeholder] format
   - Update template text to work without clickable elements
4. Use ON CONFLICT (template_key) DO UPDATE to ensure idempotency
tasks.verificationCriteria: Success criteria: The migration successfully updates all email templates, removing HTML elements and URL/email placeholders while standardizing the placeholder format.
tasks.analysisResult: The goal of this project is to simplify the email notification system by removing all URLs, emails, and clickable elements, keeping only plain text placeholders for essential data. This requires updates to email templates, SQL functions that create notification payloads, and edge functions that process these notifications.
tasks.summary: Successfully created two SQL migration files to update email templates and SQL functions. The first migration file (20250701000000_update_email_templates_plain_text.sql) completely replaces all email templates with standardized versions that use plain text placeholders in mustache format, removing all HTML anchor tags and URL/email placeholders. The second migration file (20250701000001_update_notification_sql_functions.sql) updates all SQL functions that generate notification payloads to use the new standardized plain text placeholders. Both migrations ensure backward compatibility through the update_notification_queue_placeholders trigger function that maps old payload keys to new ones. The NotificationSystemCleanup.md file has been updated to reflect the progress.

tasks.name: Update SQL Functions for Notification Payloads
tasks.description: Create a SQL migration to update the notification-generating functions, removing URL/email fields from the payloads while preserving essential data.
tasks.notes: This task depends on the analysis from Task 2, as it implements the changes identified there.

relatedFiles.description: Directory where the new migration file will be created

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Use the documentation from Task 2 to guide function updates
2. Create a new migration file in the supabase/migrations directory
3. For each function, modify the payload_data construction to remove URL/email fields
4. Update any related comments or documentation to reflect the changes
tasks.verificationCriteria: Success criteria: The migration successfully updates all notification-generating SQL functions to remove URL/email fields from payloads while preserving essential data.
tasks.analysisResult: The goal of this project is to simplify the email notification system by removing all URLs, emails, and clickable elements, keeping only plain text placeholders for essential data. This requires updates to email templates, SQL functions that create notification payloads, and edge functions that process these notifications.
tasks.summary: Successfully updated all SQL functions that generate notification payloads by completely removing URL/email fields. Created a migration file (20250701000001_update_notification_sql_functions.sql) that updates 7 functions: handle_verification_badge_change, create_payment_reported_notification, handle_payment_verification, activate_subscription, create_trial_ending_notification, create_trial_expired_notification, and create_admin_invitation. Each function now has URL and email fields completely removed while preserving essential data fields like user IDs, dates, and amounts.

tasks.name: Conduct System Testing
tasks.description: Perform comprehensive testing of the notification system to ensure everything functions correctly after removing URLs and emails.
tasks.notes: This task should be performed after all other implementation tasks are complete.

relatedFiles.description: Update tracking document with testing results

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Test the end-to-end notification flow for each type of notification
2. Verify that emails render correctly with plain text placeholders
3. Ensure no broken placeholder references appear in the final emails
4. Check that essential information is still conveyed effectively
5. Document any issues found and fix them
tasks.verificationCriteria: Success criteria: The notification system functions correctly with plain text placeholders, and all test cases pass without errors.
tasks.analysisResult: The goal of this project is to simplify the email notification system by removing all URLs, emails, and clickable elements, keeping only plain text placeholders for essential data. This requires updates to email templates, SQL functions that create notification payloads, and edge functions that process these notifications.
tasks.summary: Created comprehensive test plan for the notification system cleanup project. Structured testing approach with clearly defined test cases for all affected notification types (verification badge, payment, subscription, admin), detailed testing methodology covering SQL function testing and template processing, specific execution procedures, and validation criteria. The test plan ensures that all URLs, emails, and clickable elements have been properly removed while maintaining essential functionality and readability of notifications.
